channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Top US health agency makes $25,000 buyout offer to most of its employees
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Most of the 80,000 federal workers responsible for researching diseases, inspecting food and administering Medicare and Medicaid under the auspices of the Health and Human Services Department were emailed an offer to leave their job for as much as a $25,000 payment as part of Presi
item.dc:creator: AMANDA SEITZ, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Canadians feel ‘under economic attack,’ frustration with US over Trump tariffs, annexation talk: ambassador
item.description: Canadian Ambassador to the U.S. Kirsten Hillman said citizens "don't really appreciate" President Donald Trump's talk about annexing Canada and feel "frustrated" over his tariffs.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump DOJ's limits on FACE Act enforcement fuel concern from abortion providers
item.description: New Justice Department leaders say past enforcement of the Freedom of Access to Clinic Entrances Act is "the prototypical example" of what they call "the weaponization of law enforcement.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Lucas, MPR News

item.title: Travel headaches show need for feds to focus on airline tech jobs as true major delay culprit revealed
item.description: Airline safety is back in the news after several tragic incidents in US skies, and airline maintenance jobs are now at a premium, experts and lawmakers said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: JD Vance says he was confronted by pro-Ukrainian protesters while walking with toddler daughter
item.description: Vice President JD Vance said Saturday he was accosted by pro-Ukrainian protesters who scared his 3-year-old daughter a week after his tense exchange with the Ukrainian president.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: FEMA cancels classes at national fire training academy amid federal funding cuts
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — The country's preeminent federal fire training academy canceled classes, effective immediately, on Saturday amid the ongoing flurry of funding freezes and staffing cuts by President Donald Trump's administration.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTINE FERNANDO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Blue state mayor proposes city ordinance to strengthen transgender care protections in the face of Trump EOs
item.description: Democratic Seattle Mayor Bruce Harrell proposed city legislation this week to strengthen protections for people seeking transgender care and surgeries.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Tribes and Native American students sue over Bureau of Indian Education firings
item.description: Three tribal nations and five Native American students say in a lawsuit that the Trump administration has failed its legal obligations to tribes when it cut jobs at Bureau of Indian Education schools.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Republicans call for Trump to cut off American Bar Association
item.description: Republican senators signed a scathing letter to the American Bar Association, calling out the organization's alleged political biases against President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump will lead task force preparing for 2026 World Cup
item.description: President Donald Trump has created a task force to prepare for the 2026 World Cup, which will bring the globe's premier soccer tournament to North America at a time when his on-again, off-again tariffs have ratcheted up tensions across the continent.
item.dc:creator: JONATHAN J. COOPER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: House GOP campaign chairman says voters will 'reward us' for the Trump-Musk DOGE cuts
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Almost two months into President Donald Trump's second term, the chairman of the House Republican campaign committee is already predicting his party will pick up seats in the midterm elections some 20 months away. Rep. Richard Hudson, R-N.C.
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: White House unleashes on 'rogue bureaucrats' after agency head refuses DOGE entry to headquarters
item.description: The president of the U.S. African Development Foundation filed a lawsuit against the Department of Government Efficiency after the cost-cutting team entered the agency's office.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump order denies student loan relief to nonprofit workers engaged in 'improper' activity
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump is ordering changes to the Public Service Loan Forgiveness program that would disqualify workers of nonprofit groups deemed to have engaged in “improper” activities.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Renewed debate sparks discussion on 'My Safe Florida Home' program
item.description: Governor Ron DeSantis wants more Floridians to benefit from the "My Safe Florida Home" program, which provides storm-hardening home improvements for homeowners.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: A single day of Trump and Musk's cost-cutting campaign remakes huge sections of government
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A series of decisions revealed Friday provided a glimpse of the turmoil engulfing federal agencies since President Donald Trump and Elon Musk launched their campaign of disruption, upending how government functions in ways big and small.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Politics Friday: Moves in Washington hover over state budget outlook
item.description: MPR News senior politics editor Brian Bakst talked to Sen. Mark Johnson about the state’s budget situation. Plus, a conversation with the dean of Minnesota’s congressional delegation, U.S. Rep. Betty McCollum.
item.dc:creator: Brian Bakst and Matthew Alvarez, MPR News

item.title: Housing policies cited as major reason families forced to give up pets
item.description: Pet rescues and animal shelters hear it over and over: a family has to give up their dog because they had to move and the new place has a rule.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump threatens tariffs on Canadian dairy, lumber products as soon as Friday
item.description: Access to dairy and lumber markets have long been two of the biggest points of contention in the otherwise strong economic relationship between the U.S. and Canada.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: Elon Musk tells Republican lawmakers he's not to blame for federal firings
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — It’s not his fault. Billionaire Elon Musk is telling Republican lawmakers that he is not to blame for the firings of thousands of federal workers, including veterans, as pushes to downsize the government.
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO and CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A downbeat Minnesota budget forecast initiates lawmaker blame game
item.description: Minnesota lawmakers received an economic budget forecast that shows less wiggle room as they write a new budget and an even larger looming deficit in the future. Gov. Tim Walz and DFL leaders blamed President Donald Trump while Republican leaders said Democrats need to look in the mirror.
item.dc:creator: Clay Masters, MPR News

item.title: Trump changes course and delays some tariffs on Mexico and Canada
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump on Thursday postponed 25% tariffs on many imports from Mexico and some imports from Canada for a month amid widespread fears of the economic fallout from a broader trade war.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER RUGABER, JOSH BOAK and ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Aces could join Las Vegas teams with their own specialty license plates
item.description: Las Vegas Aces fans might get a chance to show their support as the state considers issuing a specialty license plate.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump passes Clinton, gives longest modern address to joint session of Congress
item.description: President Donald Trump gave the longest address to a joint session of Congress in modern history on Tuesday night, surpassing former President Bill Clinton’s previous mark.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, Fox 5

item.title: DeSantis' State of the State: government accountability a key focus
item.description: Gov. Ron DeSantis gave his state of the state address Tuesday and offered his plan for the year ahead. Members of the legislature, and leaders past and present gathered in Tallahassee for the speech Tuesday morning.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump threatens increased tariffs if Canada retaliates
item.description: President Donald Trump on Tuesday threatened to add additional taxes on Canada if the U.S.'s northern neighbor Canada retaliates with its own after Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau slammed Trump’s newly implemented tariffs.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5

item.title: 5 things to watch as Trump delivers joint address to Congress
item.description: President Trump will head to the Capitol on Tuesday for the first joint address of his second term emboldened by a month of mass federal agency slashing, shocking foreign policy moves and a dizzying array of executive actions that have disrupted Washington and beyond.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5

item.title: DOGE cuts all GSA employees across Nevada, senator says
item.description: President Donald Trump and Elon Musk's Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) fired all General Services Administration employees across Nevada on Monday, according to Democratic Nevada Sen. Catherine Cortez Masto.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 2 Las Vegas teens present antisemitism bill at Nevada Legislature in Carson City
item.description: Two Las Vegas high school students helped put antisemitism in the public eye on Monday as they testified at the Nevada Legislature on one bill and helped present another.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News
